title: The Comprehensive List of AI Governance Tools

tools.name: IBM watsonx.governance
tools.description: IBM watsonx.governance connects AI inventories, risks, controls, regulatory obligations, evaluations, and lifecycle workflows across machine-learning, generative-AI, and agentic systems in hybrid and multi-vendor environments.

tools.name: Credo AI
tools.description: Credo AI provides AI-system discovery, inventory, risk assessment, policy packs, control mapping, evidence workflows, and governance for models, applications, agents, and third-party AI vendors.

tools.name: Holistic AI
tools.description: Holistic AI provides enterprise AI discovery, risk and bias testing, continuous monitoring, compliance workflows, and audit evidence for models, applications, and agents.

tools.name: ModelOp Center
tools.description: ModelOp Center is an AI lifecycle management and governance platform with a system of record, role-based workflows, policy controls, integrations, and portfolio reporting for internally built and vendor AI.

tools.name: OneTrust AI Governance
tools.description: OneTrust AI Governance catalogs AI systems, assesses use-case and vendor risk, maps controls to policies and regulations, monitors posture, and records governance decisions within the OneTrust platform.

tools.name: Collibra AI Command Center
tools.description: Collibra AI Command Center combines AI and agent inventory, data lineage, risk assessment, policy workflows, trust signals, evidence management, and compliance reporting across heterogeneous AI platforms.

tools.name: Dataiku Govern
tools.description: Dataiku Govern centralizes AI and analytics initiatives in registries, applies risk qualifications and approval workflows, records audit timelines, and can block deployment until required sign-offs are complete.

tools.name: DataRobot AI Governance
tools.description: DataRobot AI Governance provides agent and model registries, lineage, approval controls, compliance documentation, runtime moderation, and policy enforcement across cloud, on-premises, edge, and air-gapped deployments.

tools.name: ServiceNow AI Control Tower
tools.description: ServiceNow AI Control Tower discovers and inventories AI assets, manages lifecycle risk and compliance, monitors runtime behavior, and connects governance actions to ServiceNow workflows and configuration data.

tools.name: SAP LeanIX AI Governance
tools.description: SAP LeanIX AI Governance maps AI technologies and agents to applications and business capabilities, tracks ownership, lifecycle, policy status, and risk, and reports the AI estate through enterprise architecture views.

tools.name: Saidot
tools.description: Saidot uses a governance knowledge graph to connect systems, models, agents, datasets, risks, controls, policies, and evidence, with inherited requirements and integrations for AI development platforms.

tools.name: Trustible
tools.description: Trustible provides AI intake, inventory, risk scoring, vendor review, control mapping, approval workflows, continuous oversight, and audit evidence for governance, legal, risk, and compliance teams.

tools.name: Asenion (formerly Fairly AI)
tools.description: Asenion (formerly Fairly AI) is the platform formed after Fairly AI acquired Anch.AI, combining AI governance, risk and compliance workflows with technical testing and assurance across the AI lifecycle.

tools.name: MLflow Model Registry
tools.category: AI Inventory, Model Registry, and Lifecycle Governance
tools.description: MLflow Model Registry provides a centralized model store with versioning, aliases, tags, annotations, experiment lineage, APIs, and a user interface for managing models from development through deployment.

tools.name: Kubeflow Hub
tools.category: AI Inventory, Model Registry, and Lifecycle Governance
tools.description: Kubeflow Hub combines the Kubeflow Model Registry for registering, versioning, and tracking model artifacts with a federated Model Catalog for discovering models from external sources.

tools.name: Weights & Biases Registry
tools.category: AI Inventory, Model Registry, and Lifecycle Governance
tools.description: Weights & Biases Registry organizes versioned models, datasets, and other artifacts into governed collections with lineage, aliases, metadata, access controls, and lifecycle automation.

tools.name: ClearML Model Registry
tools.category: AI Inventory, Model Registry, and Lifecycle Governance
tools.description: ClearML Model Registry records model artifacts, versions, metadata, framework details, task lineage, and publication state as part of ClearML's experiment and deployment lifecycle.

tools.name: DVC Studio Model Registry
tools.category: AI Inventory, Model Registry, and Lifecycle Governance
tools.description: DVC Studio Model Registry manages model versions, stages, metadata, metrics, and Git-linked lineage while using DVC-tracked artifacts as the underlying versioned model data.

tools.name: Hopsworks Model Registry
tools.category: AI Inventory, Model Registry, and Lifecycle Governance
tools.description: Hopsworks Model Registry stores versioned models with schemas, metrics, provenance, and deployment metadata, linking models to feature data and training pipelines in the Hopsworks platform.

tools.name: OpenMetadata ML Model Assets
tools.category: AI Inventory, Model Registry, and Lifecycle Governance
tools.description: OpenMetadata ML Model Assets catalog models, algorithms, features, hyperparameters, lineage, owners, tags, and test results imported from supported machine-learning platforms.

tools.name: DataHub ML Models
tools.category: AI Inventory, Model Registry, and Lifecycle Governance
tools.description: DataHub ML Models represent machine-learning models and model groups in DataHub's metadata graph, enabling ownership, lineage, documentation, discovery, and governance relationships with data assets.

tools.name: Cloudera AI Registry
tools.category: AI Inventory, Model Registry, and Lifecycle Governance
tools.description: Cloudera AI Registry catalogs, versions, and promotes models with metadata and lineage as part of Cloudera Machine Learning's governed model-development and deployment workflow.

tools.name: SAS Model Manager
tools.category: AI Inventory, Model Registry, and Lifecycle Governance
tools.description: SAS Model Manager provides model registration, versioning, validation, workflow approvals, deployment, performance monitoring, lineage, and reporting for analytical and machine-learning models.

tools.name: Atlan AI Governance
tools.category: AI Inventory, Model Registry, and Lifecycle Governance
tools.description: Atlan AI Governance catalogs AI use cases, models, prompts, and data dependencies, connects them to ownership and lineage, and applies policy and access workflows through Atlan's metadata platform.

tools.name: Optro / FairNow
tools.description: Optro / FairNow provides AI inventory, regulatory applicability guidance, risk assessments, bias testing, evidence collection, and compliance workflows; FairNow became part of Optro while its product site remained available.

tools.name: LogicGate AI Governance
tools.description: LogicGate AI Governance adds AI use-case intake, policy management, risk assessments, approvals, control mapping, and third-party oversight to the LogicGate Risk Cloud GRC platform.

tools.name: Riskonnect AI Governance
tools.description: Riskonnect AI Governance maintains AI inventories, risk and compliance assessments, approval workflows, monitoring records, and audit trails within Riskonnect's integrated enterprise risk platform.

tools.name: Archer AI Governance
tools.description: Archer AI Governance provides AI use-case and model inventories, EU AI Act-aligned risk classification, privacy and ethical impact assessments, obligations, controls, workflows, and compliance reporting.

tools.name: VerifyWise
tools.description: VerifyWise is a self-hostable or managed AI governance platform under the Business Source License 1.1, with inventories, risks, policies, evidence, incidents, evaluations, and multi-framework control mappings.

tools.name: trail
tools.description: trail provides an AI asset registry, risk classification, control workflows, evidence collection, documentation generation, and governance agents for EU AI Act, ISO/IEC 42001, and organization-specific processes.

tools.name: Enzai
tools.description: Enzai provides AI inventories, structured intake, policy packs, risk assessments, live compliance scoring, evidence, and requirement-level mappings for the EU AI Act, NIST AI RMF, ISO/IEC 42001, and other regimes.

tools.name: Modulos
tools.description: Modulos connects frameworks, requirements, controls, risks, and evidence in a governance graph, supporting AI inventories, cross-framework mappings, assessments, and audit preparation.

tools.name: Regulativ AI Governor
tools.description: Regulativ AI Governor provides an AI asset registry, risk classification, policy packs, continuous monitoring, impact assessments, conformity workflows, and evidence exports for multiple regulatory frameworks.

tools.name: Govern365
tools.description: Govern365 provides an AI system registry, framework-specific risk assessments, compliance dashboards, policy and control templates, incident tracking, and audit evidence management.

tools.name: MetricStream AI for GRC
tools.description: MetricStream AI for GRC provides governed model access, prompt and data controls, audit logging, model observability, evaluation pipelines, and human review within MetricStream's risk and compliance products.

tools.name: Monitaur
tools.description: Monitaur combines policy and control management, model validation, production monitoring, decision records, and evidence mapping to support audit-ready assurance across traditional, generative, and agentic AI.

tools.name: ValidMind
tools.description: ValidMind provides model inventories, documentation automation, validation test plans, independent-review workflows, approvals, findings, monitoring triggers, and audit trails for AI and model risk management.

tools.name: Giskard
tools.description: Giskard tests machine-learning and language-model applications for quality, bias, security, and robustness, and produces structured scan results and test suites that can support assurance evidence.

tools.name: Confident AI
tools.description: Confident AI is an AI quality platform for enterprise platform teams to define one standard across teams; AI governance gates enforce eval and red-team requirements before launch and continuously in production.

tools.name: DeepEval
tools.description: DeepEval is an open-source LLM evaluation framework that runs repeatable end-to-end and component-level tests locally or in CI, producing test results that can serve as technical evidence for quality controls.

tools.name: DeepTeam
tools.description: DeepTeam is an open-source LLM red-teaming framework that simulates jailbreak, prompt-injection, and multi-turn attacks, maps tests to named safety frameworks, and exports structured risk-assessment results.

tools.name: Inspect AI
tools.description: Inspect AI is the UK AI Security Institute's framework for reproducible model evaluations, with task, solver, scorer, sandbox, logging, and approval primitives for safety and capability testing.

tools.name: promptfoo
tools.description: promptfoo runs configurable evaluations and adversarial tests for LLM applications in local development and CI, with assertions, comparisons, vulnerability scans, and shareable result reports.

tools.name: NIST Dioptra
tools.description: NIST Dioptra is a test platform for measuring, tracking, and comparing AI risks, including model robustness to adversarial attacks, through reproducible experiments and extensible workflows.

tools.name: AI Verify Testing Framework
tools.description: AI Verify Testing Framework is an open-source toolkit and workbench for running technical tests, process checks, and reporting against responsible-AI principles and selected governance requirements.

tools.name: METR Vivaria
tools.description: METR Vivaria is an open-source platform for running agent evaluations in isolated task environments, recording trajectories, scoring outcomes, and supporting human review of evaluation evidence.

tools.name: PyRIT
tools.description: PyRIT is Microsoft's open-source framework for orchestrating generative-AI red teaming, recording prompts and responses, applying scorers, and reproducing attack campaigns across target systems.

tools.name: Fiddler
tools.description: Fiddler evaluates and monitors predictive models, generative-AI applications, and agents for performance, drift, hallucination, policy violations, bias, and other configurable quality or risk signals.

tools.name: Arthur
tools.description: Arthur provides model and generative-AI monitoring, evaluations, explainability, drift and performance metrics, and runtime guardrails; its open-source Arthur Engine supplies selected local capabilities.

tools.name: Arize AI
tools.description: Arize AI provides production observability, evaluation, tracing, drift detection, performance analysis, and troubleshooting for predictive models, computer vision, language models, and AI agents.

tools.name: Coralogix AI Center
tools.description: Coralogix AI Center incorporates Aporia technology and provides AI monitoring, guardrails, evaluations, security-posture discovery, and telemetry for language-model applications and coding agents.

tools.name: Evidently
tools.description: Evidently is an open-source-first framework for evaluating, testing, and monitoring data, machine-learning models, and LLM applications with reports, metrics, test suites, and dashboards.

tools.name: NannyML
tools.description: NannyML estimates post-deployment model performance when targets are delayed or absent, detects multivariate and univariate drift, and supports root-cause analysis and monitoring workflows.

tools.name: Deepchecks
tools.description: Deepchecks validates data and models across development and production with test suites for integrity, distribution change, leakage, drift, performance, and selected LLM behaviors.

tools.name: Superwise
tools.description: Superwise monitors machine-learning systems for data and concept drift, performance, bias, anomalies, and operational health, with alerts and workflows for investigation and remediation.

tools.name: Mona
tools.description: Mona monitors machine-learning and AI systems using configurable segments and metrics for performance, drift, bias, anomalies, data quality, and business-impact signals.

tools.name: Alibi Detect
tools.description: Alibi Detect is an open-source Python library for outlier, adversarial, and distribution-drift detection across tabular, text, image, and time-series data.

tools.name: whylogs
tools.description: whylogs is an open-source data-logging library that creates mergeable statistical profiles for monitoring data quality and distribution changes without retaining raw records.

tools.name: H2O MLOps
tools.description: H2O MLOps manages model deployment, approval, lineage, monitoring, drift, performance, and lifecycle operations for models built with H2O tools or external frameworks.

tools.name: SHAP
tools.description: SHAP is an open-source library based on Shapley values for explaining model outputs with local and global feature attributions across many model families.

tools.name: LIME
tools.description: LIME is an open-source library that explains individual predictions by fitting interpretable local surrogate models around the prediction being examined.

tools.name: Captum
tools.description: Captum is PyTorch's model-interpretability library with attribution methods for features, layers, neurons, and examples across vision, text, and other differentiable models.

tools.name: InterpretML
tools.description: InterpretML provides glass-box models and post-hoc explanation methods, including Explainable Boosting Machines and integrations for local and global model interpretation.

tools.name: Alibi Explain
tools.description: Alibi Explain provides model-inspection algorithms for feature attribution, counterfactuals, anchors, prototypes, and other local or global explanation methods.

tools.name: AI Explainability 360
tools.description: AI Explainability 360 is an open-source toolkit of explanation algorithms, metrics, tutorials, and guidance for interpreting machine-learning models and their predictions.

tools.name: DALEX
tools.description: DALEX provides model-agnostic explanations and diagnostics in Python and R, including variable importance, partial dependence, residual analysis, and fairness checks.

tools.name: OmniXAI
tools.description: OmniXAI is an open-source explanation library for tabular, image, text, and time-series models with multiple explanation methods and interactive dashboards.

tools.name: Xplique
tools.description: Xplique is an open-source neural-network explainability library with attribution, concept, example-based, feature-visualization, and explanation-quality methods.

tools.name: ELI5
tools.description: ELI5 is an open-source Python package for inspecting model weights, feature importances, text classifiers, and individual predictions from supported machine-learning libraries.

tools.name: Fairlearn
tools.description: Fairlearn is an open-source Python package for assessing group fairness with disparity metrics and dashboards and mitigating selected harms through reduction and post-processing algorithms.

tools.name: AI Fairness 360
tools.description: AI Fairness 360 is an open-source toolkit of fairness metrics, explanatory examples, and bias-mitigation algorithms for datasets and machine-learning models.

tools.name: Aequitas
tools.description: Aequitas is an open-source bias-audit toolkit for measuring disparities in risk scores or decisions across demographic groups and reporting group-level fairness metrics.

tools.name: TensorFlow Fairness Indicators
tools.description: TensorFlow Fairness Indicators computes and visualizes model-performance metrics across user-defined slices, including confidence intervals and comparisons between model versions.

tools.name: Responsibly
tools.description: Responsibly is an open-source toolkit for measuring and mitigating selected fairness issues in data, models, and word embeddings, with notebooks and educational workflows.

tools.name: FAT Forensics
tools.description: FAT Forensics provides interoperable fairness, accountability, and transparency methods for datasets, predictive models, and individual predictions.

tools.name: VerifyML
tools.description: VerifyML is an open-source toolkit for responsible-AI testing and documentation, including fairness tests, model-card generation, and examples for common machine-learning workflows.

tools.name: REVISE
tools.description: REVISE is an open-source tool for discovering representation, annotation, and geography-related patterns that can reveal potential bias in visual datasets.

tools.name: Privacy Meter
tools.description: Privacy Meter is an open-source toolkit for auditing machine-learning models with privacy attacks, including configurable membership-inference tests and empirical privacy-risk reports.

tools.name: Microsoft Presidio
tools.description: Microsoft Presidio detects, analyzes, anonymizes, and de-anonymizes personally identifiable information in text, images, and structured data through extensible recognizers and operators.

tools.name: Opacus
tools.description: Opacus is a PyTorch library for training models with differential privacy, including per-sample gradients, privacy accounting, clipping, and noise mechanisms.

tools.name: TensorFlow Privacy
tools.description: TensorFlow Privacy provides differentially private optimizers, privacy accounting, and attacks for training and evaluating TensorFlow machine-learning models.

tools.name: IBM Differential Privacy Library
tools.description: IBM Differential Privacy Library implements differentially private mechanisms, models, tools, and accounting interfaces compatible with familiar Python data-science workflows.

tools.name: Google Differential Privacy
tools.description: Google Differential Privacy provides libraries and building blocks for privacy-preserving statistical aggregation in C++, Go, Java, PostgreSQL, and related environments.

tools.name: OpenDP
tools.description: OpenDP is an open-source library and community project for constructing, analyzing, and releasing statistical computations with formal differential-privacy guarantees.

tools.name: SmartNoise SDK
tools.description: SmartNoise SDK provides differential-privacy mechanisms, SQL query processing, synthetic-data components, and privacy accounting built with the OpenDP ecosystem.

tools.name: PySyft
tools.description: PySyft is an open-source platform for analyzing data held by another party under access, privacy, and disclosure controls without transferring the underlying datasets.

tools.name: Fides
tools.description: Fides is an open-source privacy-engineering platform for data mapping, policy enforcement, consent, privacy requests, and governance metadata across applications and infrastructure.

tools.name: Hugging Face Model Cards
tools.description: Hugging Face Model Cards are Markdown documents with structured metadata, intended uses, limitations, datasets, licenses, and evaluation results that accompany model repositories on the Hugging Face Hub.

tools.name: AWS AI Service Cards
tools.description: AWS AI Service Cards are first-party transparency documents describing intended uses, limitations, responsible-design choices, and deployment or performance considerations for selected AWS AI services and models.

tools.name: Microsoft Transparency Notes
tools.description: Microsoft Transparency Notes are first-party documents explaining how selected Microsoft AI technologies work, their capabilities and limitations, and choices that affect system performance and behavior.

tools.name: Google DeepMind Model Cards
tools.description: Google DeepMind Model Cards are first-party structured reports describing how selected Gemini, Gemma, and generative models were designed, evaluated, and bounded.

tools.name: Anthropic System Cards
tools.description: Anthropic System Cards are first-party reports on Claude model capabilities, safety evaluations, identified risks, mitigations, and responsible deployment decisions.

tools.name: OpenAI System Cards
tools.description: OpenAI System Cards are first-party deployment-safety reports describing model capabilities, evaluation results, risk classifications, and mitigations under OpenAI's safety and preparedness processes.

tools.name: Meta System Cards
tools.description: Meta System Cards are first-party transparency documents explaining components, data use, ranking or generation processes, limitations, and user controls in selected Meta AI systems.

tools.name: Cohere Model Cards
tools.description: Cohere Model Cards are first-party documents describing model specifications, intended and unintended uses, language coverage, safety evaluations, limitations, and responsible-use considerations.

tools.name: IBM AI FactSheets 360
tools.description: IBM AI FactSheets 360 is a first-party methodology and resource collection for documenting model purpose, performance, datasets, characteristics, governance context, and stakeholder-specific facts.

tools.name: CyclOps Model Report Cards
tools.description: CyclOps Model Report Cards are generated through the open-source CyclOps reporting API to document performance, slices, thresholds, and periodic evaluation results for clinical machine-learning models.

tools.name: Palo Alto Networks Prisma AIRS
tools.category: AI Security, Guardrails, and Policy Enforcement
tools.description: Palo Alto Networks Prisma AIRS provides AI asset discovery, posture management, model scanning, automated red teaming, and runtime protection; Protect AI was acquired in July 2025 and integrated into Prisma AIRS.

tools.name: F5 AI Security Platform
tools.category: AI Security, Guardrails, and Policy Enforcement
tools.description: F5 AI Security Platform provides AI discovery, red teaming, runtime guardrails, and policy enforcement; F5 acquired CalypsoAI in 2025 and incorporated its technology into the platform.

tools.name: Cisco AI Defense
tools.category: AI Security, Guardrails, and Policy Enforcement
tools.description: Cisco AI Defense validates models and applications, discovers shadow AI, and applies runtime protection; Robust Intelligence was acquired in 2024 and became core technology for the product.

tools.name: Lakera Guard (Check Point)
tools.category: AI Security, Guardrails, and Policy Enforcement
tools.description: Lakera Guard (Check Point) provides runtime protection against prompt injection, data leakage, unsafe content, and agent threats; Check Point completed its acquisition of Lakera in October 2025.

tools.name: HiddenLayer
tools.category: AI Security, Guardrails, and Policy Enforcement
tools.description: HiddenLayer provides AI asset discovery, model scanning, threat detection, automated red teaming, and runtime defense for machine-learning models and AI applications.

tools.name: Mindgard
tools.category: AI Security, Guardrails, and Policy Enforcement
tools.description: Mindgard provides automated security testing, attack simulation, risk reporting, and continuous assessment for models, language-model applications, and AI agents.

tools.name: Bosch AIShield
tools.category: AI Security, Guardrails, and Policy Enforcement
tools.description: Bosch AIShield provides model vulnerability analysis, adversarial testing, supply-chain scanning, runtime protection, and security monitoring for machine-learning and generative-AI systems.

tools.name: NVIDIA NeMo Guardrails
tools.category: AI Security, Guardrails, and Policy Enforcement
tools.description: NVIDIA NeMo Guardrails is an open-source framework for defining programmable conversational, topical, safety, security, retrieval, and execution rails around LLM applications.

tools.name: Guardrails AI
tools.category: AI Security, Guardrails, and Policy Enforcement
tools.description: Guardrails AI is an open-source framework for validating, correcting, and constraining language-model inputs and outputs with reusable validators and structured-output specifications.

tools.name: Open Policy Agent
tools.category: AI Security, Guardrails, and Policy Enforcement
tools.description: Open Policy Agent is a general-purpose policy engine that evaluates declarative Rego policies for authorization and admission decisions, including controls around AI services and agents.

tools.name: LLM Guard
tools.category: AI Security, Guardrails, and Policy Enforcement
tools.description: LLM Guard is an open-source library of input and output scanners for prompt injection, sensitive data, toxicity, malicious links, relevance, and other language-model risks.

tools.name: ModelScan
tools.category: AI Security, Guardrails, and Policy Enforcement
tools.description: ModelScan is an open-source static scanner for detecting unsafe code and serialization patterns in machine-learning model files before loading or deployment.

tools.name: garak
tools.category: AI Security, Guardrails, and Policy Enforcement
tools.description: garak is an open-source vulnerability scanner that probes language models and applications for prompt injection, data leakage, misinformation, toxicity, jailbreaks, and other failure modes.

tools.name: Zenity
tools.category: AI Security, Guardrails, and Policy Enforcement
tools.description: Zenity discovers and assesses enterprise AI agents, copilots, and low-code applications, monitors their identities and actions, and applies security and governance controls.

tools.name: Microsoft Responsible AI Dashboard
tools.description: Microsoft Responsible AI Dashboard combines error analysis, interpretability, fairness assessment, counterfactuals, and causal analysis for supported Azure Machine Learning tabular models, with exportable scorecards.

tools.name: Azure AI Content Safety
tools.description: Azure AI Content Safety provides APIs and a studio for harmful-content classification, prompt-attack detection, groundedness checks, protected-material detection, custom categories, and blocklists.

tools.name: Microsoft Purview Data Security Posture Management
tools.description: Microsoft Purview Data Security Posture Management discovers AI use and associated data risks, applies data-security policies, and supports investigation of sensitive-data interactions across supported AI applications and agents.

tools.name: Amazon Bedrock Guardrails
tools.description: Amazon Bedrock Guardrails applies configurable content, denied-topic, sensitive-information, word, and contextual-grounding safeguards to model inputs and responses through Bedrock or the ApplyGuardrail API.

tools.name: Amazon SageMaker Clarify
tools.description: Amazon SageMaker Clarify analyzes pre-training and post-training bias, computes feature attributions, evaluates foundation models, and integrates selected bias monitoring with SageMaker Model Monitor.

tools.name: Amazon SageMaker Model Cards
tools.description: Amazon SageMaker Model Cards stores standardized model details, intended uses, risk ratings, training and evaluation results, approval status, and lifecycle documentation for audit and reporting.

tools.name: Amazon SageMaker Model Registry
tools.description: Amazon SageMaker Model Registry catalogs model versions and metadata, groups related packages, records approval status, and supports controlled promotion and deployment workflows.

tools.name: Vertex Explainable AI
tools.description: Vertex Explainable AI computes feature attributions for supported tabular, image, and custom models and exposes explanations through batch and online prediction workflows.

tools.name: Vertex AI Model Monitoring
tools.description: Vertex AI Model Monitoring runs on-demand or scheduled checks for model and data quality, skew, drift, and feature-attribution changes, with metrics and alerts.

tools.name: Google Cloud Model Armor
tools.description: Google Cloud Model Armor screens prompts, responses, and agent interactions for prompt injection, jailbreaks, harmful content, malicious URLs, and sensitive-data exposure through APIs and integrations.

tools.name: Vertex AI Model Registry
tools.description: Vertex AI Model Registry stores model versions, aliases, descriptions, labels, evaluation metadata, and deployment relationships in a central Vertex AI repository.

tools.name: Databricks Unity Catalog AI Governance
tools.description: Databricks Unity Catalog AI Governance applies cataloging, lineage, access controls, discovery, monitoring, and audit logs to data, features, models, functions, and other AI assets.

tools.name: Snowflake Horizon Catalog
tools.description: Snowflake Horizon Catalog provides discovery, lineage, access governance, policy enforcement, quality monitoring, and audit context for data and AI assets within Snowflake.

tools.name: OCI Generative AI Guardrails
tools.description: OCI Generative AI Guardrails applies configurable content moderation and prompt-injection controls to inputs and outputs for supported Oracle Cloud generative-AI workflows.

tools.name: Protect AI
tools.description: Protect AI was acquired by Palo Alto Networks on July 22, 2025, and its platform technology and team were integrated into Prisma AIRS; selected open-source repositories remain available.

tools.name: CalypsoAI
tools.description: CalypsoAI was acquired by F5 in 2025, and its runtime defense, red-teaming, and governance technology became part of the F5 AI Security Platform.

tools.name: Robust Intelligence
tools.description: Robust Intelligence was acquired by Cisco in 2024, and its technology became foundational to Cisco AI Defense and Cisco Foundation AI.

tools.name: Aporia
tools.description: Aporia was acquired by Coralogix in December 2024, and its technology and team were integrated into Coralogix AI Center and the Coralogix AI research organization.

tools.name: Fairly AI
tools.description: Fairly AI acquired Anch.AI in June 2025 and launched the combined company and platform under the Asenion name.

tools.name: Anch.AI
tools.description: Anch.AI was acquired by Fairly AI in June 2025 and combined with it to form Asenion.

tools.name: Google Model Card Toolkit
tools.description: Google Model Card Toolkit was archived on September 27, 2024; its TFX pipeline component moved to TFX Addons, while the archived library remains readable.

tools.name: WhyLabs Platform
tools.description: WhyLabs Platform discontinued hosted operations; the open-source whylogs and LangKit repositories remain available separately.
